channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: We just got another big hint that the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7 is on the way
item.description: A new version of One UI 7 has appeared, which seems to be specifically for Samsung's upcoming foldable.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: INE Security’s Cybersecurity and IT Training Enhances Career Stability in Tech
item.description: 2025 marks a time of unprecedented volatility in the technology job market. On one hand, dependence on technology is soaring. The growth of AI and machine learning is propelling a surge in new technologies, tactics, and ideas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump’s tariffs on China could risk access to rare earth elements widely used in tech sector
item.description: Lithium and other elements that the U.S. needs for vital defense, energy, and other applications could become harder to obtain if the Trump administration goes ahead with tariffs and other protectionist policies aimed at China.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Climate change is shrinking glaciers faster than ever, with 7 trillion tons lost since 2000
item.description: A new and massive study finds that climate change is melting the world’s mountain glaciers faster than ever.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hackers are targeting Signal with new QR code-linked cyberattack
item.description: Russian hackers are targeting military personnel with advanced phishing attacks as they look to access their Signal accounts.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Revered turntable maker Pro-Ject just unveiled a complete vinyl package with amp and speakers, and I love its boldness
item.description: Pro-Ject has unveiled a pair new, ‘ready-made’ audio systems for vinyl fans that go big on colour and small on complexity.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Your iPhone 15 Pro will get a Visual Intelligence upgrade soon – here’s why that’s a big deal
item.description: The iPhone 15 Pro could be getting Apple's Visual Intelligence feature in a future update, and that means there's no reason to upgrade for better AI.
item.dc:creator: john-anthony.disotto@futurenet.com (John-Anthony Disotto), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft updates VS Code C# Dev Kit extension
item.description: Microsoft announced updates to its C# Dev Kit for Visual Studio Code extension, including enhancements to Razor/Blazor Hot Reload and for debugging and testing Blazor web pages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO
item.description: Katie Arrington's return to DoD comes as her lawyers work out a settlement with the government related to the suspension of her security clearance in 2021. The post Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Venture Capital kills ‘more value than it creates,’ Bay Area author says
item.description: In a new book, World Eaters, tech activist Catherine Bracy says Silicon Valley needs to focus more on solving real-world problems and less on fast profit.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Smart home device maker exposes 2.7 billion records in huge data breach
item.description: Major IoT data breach: Mars Hydro database with 2.7 billion records exposed. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the breach is a reminder of the risks that come with using IoT devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A comprehensive look at DOGE’s firings and layoffs so far
item.description: Thousands of federal government employees have been shown the door in the first month of Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: 5 ways to fix OneDrive’s worst annoyances on Windows
item.description: On Windows, OneDrive is built right into the operating system. Even if you don’t turn it on, it will pop up occasionally, asking you to “back up your files to the cloud.” What that really means, of course, is that Microsoft wants you to move your folders to OneDrive. I’m not here to knock OneDrive.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Office 2021 and 2024 cheat sheet
item.description: For decades, Microsoft Office has been the business world’s dominant productivity software suite. If you use word processing, spreadsheet, presentation, and email applications, particularly on Windows, those apps are very likely to be Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Generative AI vs. the software developer
item.description: If you aren’t aware of the tectonic shift that is generative artificial intelligence, then I can only assume your phone has been on Airplane mode for the last year. You can’t swing a laptop bag these days without knocking over four startups doing something with AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to use asyncio: Python’s built-in async library
item.description: Python’s asynchronous programming functionality, or async for short, allows you to write programs that get more work done by not waiting for independent tasks to finish. The asyncio library is a set of tools included with Python for asynchronously processing disk or network I/O.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 4 takeaways from the Trump, Elon Musk interview
item.description: President Trump and tech billionaire Elon Musk sat down for a joint interview with Fox News’s Sean Hannity and defended the administration’s massive overhaul of the federal government while giving some insights into their friendship.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Airbnb sues New Orleans after it adopts sweeping regulations governing short-term rentals
item.description: Airbnb is suing the City of New Orleans for requiring the company and other short-term rental platforms ensure properties they market are in compliance with city regulations.
item.dc:creator: Jack Brook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: National Science Foundation cuts 10 percent of staff
item.description: Nearly 170 employees at the National Science Foundation (NSF) who were still on probation were fired by the Trump administration on Tuesday, union officials told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: How drones may be a big help to first responders in the future
item.description: A recent challenge sponsored by NIST tasked drone designers with creating a system to help first responders map indoor environments. The post How drones may be a big help to first responders in the future first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Mexico Awaits Google Response on Gulf Name Dispute
item.description: Mexico said Monday it's awaiting a new response from Google to its request that the tech company fully restore the name Gulf of Mexico to its Google Maps service before filing a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mac users beware: AI-powered malware threats are on the rise
item.description: Even Apple products are vulnerable to cybercriminals, and a new report suggests the owners of Mac products will have to be more vigilant this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk shared Mars plan over drinks, Star Trek convo: Entrepreneur
item.description: "I realized he was dead serious. Elon Musk doesn't just dream, he makes things happen," said entrepreneur and academic Vivek Wadhwa.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, Fox 5
